title: Init preserves existing global and project config files
description: Black-box smoke for `orca init` when both target config files already exist.
input: Seed both global and project config files with sentinel values, run `orca init`, and verify the command does not overwrite the existing configs.
command:
bash -lc 'REPO_ROOT=${PROJECT_DIR:-$(pwd)}; cd "$REPO_ROOT"; bash agent-eval/scripts/prepare-fast-gate.sh >/dev/null; TMP_HOME=$(mktemp -d); TMP_PROJECT=$(mktemp -d); trap "rm -rf $TMP_HOME $TMP_PROJECT" EXIT; mkdir -p "$TMP_HOME/.orca"; cat > "$TMP_HOME/.orca/config.json" <<EOF
{"providers":{"openai":{"apiKey":"keep-me"}},"defaultProvider":"openai"}
EOF
cat > "$TMP_PROJECT/.orca.json" <<EOF
{"providers":{"poe":{"apiKey":"keep-project"}},"defaultProvider":"poe"}
EOF
OUTPUT=$(cd "$TMP_PROJECT" && env -i PATH="$PATH" HOME="$TMP_HOME" ORCA_HOME="$TMP_HOME/.orca" node "$REPO_ROOT/dist/bin/orca.js" init); printf "%s\n" "$OUTPUT"; grep -q "keep-me" "$TMP_HOME/.orca/config.json"; grep -q "keep-project" "$TMP_PROJECT/.orca.json"; printf "init-preserve-ok\n"'
